segments.text: When Samuel grew old, he appointed his sons as Israel’s leaders.
The name of his firstborn was Joel and the name of his second was Abijah, and they served at Beersheba.
But his sons did not follow his ways. They turned aside after dishonest gain and accepted bribes and perverted justice.
So all the elders of Israel gathered together and came to Samuel at Ramah.
They said to him,
You are old, and your sons do not follow your ways; now appoint a king to lead us, such as all the other nations have.
segments.speaker: Elders of Israel

segments.text: But when they said,
Give us a king to lead us,” this displeased Samuel; so he prayed to the Lord.
segments.speaker: Elders of Israel

segments.text: And the Lord told him:
Listen to all that the people are saying to you; it is not you they have rejected, but they have rejected me as their king.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: As they have done from the day I brought them up out of Egypt until this day, forsaking me and serving other gods, so they are doing to you.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Now listen to them; but warn them solemnly and let them know what the king who will reign over them will claim as his rights.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Samuel told all the words of the Lord to the people who were asking him for a king.
He said,
This is what the king who will reign over you will claim as his rights: He will take your sons and make them serve with his chariots and horses, and they will run in front of his chariots.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Some he will assign to be commanders of thousands and commanders of fifties, and others to plow his ground and reap his harvest, and still others to make weapons of war and equipment for his chariots.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: He will take your daughters to be perfumers and cooks and bakers.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: He will take the best of your fields and vineyards and olive groves and give them to his attendants.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: He will take a tenth of your grain and of your vintage and give it to his officials and attendants.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Your male and female servants and the best of your cattle and donkeys he will take for his own use.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: He will take a tenth of your flocks, and you yourselves will become his slaves.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: When that day comes, you will cry out for relief from the king you have chosen, but the Lord will not answer you in that day.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: But the people refused to listen to Samuel.
No!
segments.speaker: The people

segments.text: We want a king over us.
segments.speaker: The people

segments.text: Then we will be like all the other nations, with a king to lead us and to go out before us and fight our battles.
segments.speaker: The people

segments.text: When Samuel heard all that the people said, he repeated it before the Lord.
The Lord answered,
Listen to them and give them a king.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then Samuel said to the Israelites,
Everyone go back to your own town.
segments.speaker: Samuel
